title: Tool dispatch registry pattern + 73→~40 consolidation + weekly self-prune
current_state: agent/tools.py is 1681 lines, mostly one if/elif tool_name == 'X': ladder (see lines 1400-1672 for the calendar/media/browser/computer-use/watcher cluster). Adding a new tool requires (1) new schema entry in get_tool_definitions(), (2) new dispatch branch in execute_tool(), (3) new import or _lazy() factory line, (4) verify pass. Schemas and implementations live in different functions and drift. Tool count is 73 — past planner diminishing returns (~25-30 tool ceiling for clean selection). S-055 deprecated knowledge_graph layer at 0 invocations over 24 days; same audit will surface more dead tools.
structural_problem: Tool sprawl degrades planner accuracy because the model picks from 73 schemas every turn. Hand-rolled dispatch makes refactor friction so high that the code resists improvement. Categories of redundancy: 5 media analyzers, 6 fetchers (web/reddit/discord/briefing), 4 watcher ops, 5 computer-use + 8 browser-auto with overlapping intent.
target_state: Each tools/tools_*.py exports TOOLS = [{name, schema, fn}]. agent/tools.py::execute_tool is a dict lookup (~5 lines). agent/tools.py::get_tool_definitions is a list comprehension over modules. agent/tools.py drops from 1681 to ~150 lines. Tool count drops from 73 to ~40 via merges. A weekly cron audits invocation counts and files P3 prune tickets for unused tools.
files_affected: tools/tools_memory.py, tools_execution.py, tools_awareness.py, tools_project.py, tools_web.py, tools_obsidian.py, tools_image.py, tools_gmail.py, tools_calendar.py, tools_media.py, tools_browse.py, tools_browser_auto.py, tools_computer_use.py, tools_telegram.py, tools_tts.py, tools_stt.py, tools_scheduler.py (each gains TOOLS export)
prompts/consciousness.txt (update tool name references for merged tools)
success_criteria: R2.1: get_tool_definitions() returns same schema list as before refactor (count + shapes match per regression test)
R2.1: new tool addition requires only editing the owning module (1-line TOOLS append)
R2.2: alias mapping in registry preserves old tool names for 1 release cycle, then removed in followup ticket
R2.2: solutions log entry per merge with before/after invocation examples
R2.3: Friday 02:00 local cron runs scripts/passive/tool_usage_audit.py
R2.3: tools with <3 invocations in 30 days auto-file P3 prune ticket
R2.3: tools with >50% failure rate over >10 invocations auto-file P2 fix ticket
risk_notes: Mechanical but touches every tool module. Mitigation: complete R2.1 (registry shape, no merges) in its own PR, ship, then R2.2 merges in followup PRs. R2.3 cron is independent.
migration_plan: 1. Write docs/adr/002-tool-registry-pattern.md documenting the TOOLS export contract
2. R2.1: pick one module (e.g. tools_memory.py); add TOOLS export; teach agent/tools.py to consume it; verify get_tool_definitions() output unchanged
3. R2.1: extend to all modules one by one; collapse execute_tool elif ladder per module as each TOOLS export lands
4. R2.1: delete the now-unreachable elif branches; archive snapshot of old agent/tools.py to docs/_archive/
5. R2.2 merger A: analyze_image/images/video/ocr_image/analyze_document_smart → analyze_media(path[s], question, kind='auto')
6. R2.2 merger B: web_browse/reddit_browse/reddit_search/reddit_thread/discord_read → fetch(source, query_or_url, options); keep daily_briefing as separate workflow
8. R2.2 audit D: computer_run_task removed from tool registry, moved to scripts/sprint.py command (it is a recursive agent, not a tool)
10. Append SOLUTIONS.jsonl entries for R2.1, R2.2, R2.3 separately
progress_note: R2.1 shipped 2026-05-17 (S-060). 74 tools migrated to ToolSpec registry, agent/tools.py 1681→235 lines. R2.2 (4 mergers) and R2.3 (audit cron) remain open.
